content.text:
#780 (flake fix) is merged. Stream C's verdict, in short:

**UC dbt lineage: live-verified PASS.** A real `dbt-databricks` build (dbt-core 1.11 + adapter 1.12, manifest v12) created two probe views over the live `dataq_retail` catalog; DataQ ingested the manifest through the real `refresh_dbt_lineage` task and produced the correct 2-edge chain. The two properties that mattered both held:

- **Identity unification** — the run-stamped asset from a UC suite and the dbt-refresh node for the same table resolved to the *same asset row* (namespace `unitycatalog://dbc-….cloud.databricks.com`, byte-identical), with the suite connection's provenance preserved.
- **Blast radius** — `downstream_assets(raw.sales_events)` returned both dbt models; upstream walk correct in reverse.

No product bug found, so nothing filed. One environment note: the local dev Postgres was three migrations behind head (the agent ran `alembic upgrade head` to fix it — that's why its asset queries initially errored). Everything else was cleaned up: probe schemas dropped in Databricks, probe suite/connection/assets deleted locally, the harness probe project removed.

That closes item #4 — the matrix's "adapter-aware but not live-tested" caveat for UC is now discharged. Streams A (#773) and B (#768) are still building; I'll review and land their PRs when they finish.

The #781 review surfaced two real findings (the reviewer ran GX live to check): the pandas-path guidance is factually wrong for UC/CSV string columns (they're plain `object` dtype — no Arrow backend — so both `'object'` and `'str'` pass, via different GX paths), and worse, on a wrong guess there GX returns **no `observed_value` at all**, so the PR's own "read the observed_value" calibration tip dead-ends. Plus a missing nullable-BIGINT → `float64` upcast caveat. Sending both back to the builder to fix in-PR:

input.summary: Review findings on PR #781 — fix in-PR
input.message:
The adversarial review of your PR #781 confirmed two factual findings by running GX 1.17.2 live. Fix both in-PR from your worktree (fixup commit + plain push — never amend/force-push), then re-run the frontend gate (+ backend pytest for the docs? not needed if only text/docs/frontend change) and report back.

FINDING 1 (high) — the pandas-path hint + docs are wrong for UC/CSV string columns, and the calibration tip dead-ends there:
- unity_catalog.py:151 (`pd.read_sql_table`) and flatfile.py:117 (`pd.read_csv`) do NOT use the Arrow backend (unlike parquet/iceberg), so string columns land as plain pandas `object` dtype.
- Verified GX behavior on an object-dtype column: `type_='object'` passes via the aggregate dtype path (observed_value='object_'); `type_='str'` ALSO passes but via GX's row-wise fallback (when actual dtype is object_ and type_ isn't object/object_/O, GX routes to row-wise per-value comparison). So the current text "str, not object" is backwards/misleading.
- Worse: a WRONG guess on that row-wise path (e.g. type_='int64' on a string column) produces a failing result with NO observed_value key at all (gx_runner.py:205-206 maps that to observed_value=None), and DryRunPreview renders Observed as an em-dash — so the "read the failing result's observed_value" workflow yields nothing exactly there.

Fix: rewrite the pandas-path hint + the docs cheat-sheet to be accurate, e.g.: numeric columns report numpy dtypes (`int64`, `float64`, `bool`); string columns on Unity Catalog and CSV are pandas `object` dtype — enter `object` (exact dtype match) or a Python value type name like `str` (row-wise match), both pass; Parquet/Iceberg are Arrow-backed and report Arrow-flavored names — use the dry-run's observed_value there. And qualify the calibration tip honestly: on Snowflake and Arrow-backed sources the failing result's observed_value shows the exact expected string; on UC/CSV a wrong value-type guess falls to row-wise comparison and shows NO observed value — if Observed renders as "—", switch to `object` or the Python value type name.

FINDING 2 (medium) — nullable-int upcast caveat missing: `pd.read_sql_table`/`read_csv` upcast an integer column containing ANY NULL to `float64` (verified live). The doc's flat "BIGINT → int64" claim breaks for nullable columns. Add the caveat to both the doc table row and (briefly) the hint: "integer columns containing NULLs report `float64`".

Keep the hint text concise (it renders under a form field — 2-3 sentences max; the full nuance goes in the docs cheat-sheet, which the hint can point to). Update your RTL tests for the new wording (avoid brittle full-string matches — match on a stable substring). Re-run: prettier, eslint, pnpm test:coverage; plus black/ruff on nothing unless backend touched. Push the fixup to fix/issue-768-type-hint and reply with the final wording used and gate results.

The #782 review surfaced five findings, led by a real race: OwnerBlock's admin-users fetch captures `canEdit` at first effect run, so an admin deep-linking into an asset page before `/me` resolves never gets the picker populated. Sending all five back to the builder:

input.summary: Five review findings on PR #782 — fix in-PR
input.message:
The adversarial review of your PR #782 confirmed five findings. Fix all in-PR from your worktree (fixup commit + plain push — never amend/force-push), re-run the frontend gate, and report back with what you changed.

1. HIGH — OwnerBlock admin-users fetch race: useAsyncData's effect deps are [nonce] only, and your fetcher closes over `canEdit`. `useIsWorkspaceAdmin()` is false until /me resolves, and AuthGate renders children before /me resolves — so an admin deep-linking/refreshing into /assets/:id mounts OwnerBlock with canEdit=false, the fetch is skipped forever (no dep re-fires it, nothing calls its reload), the Select stays empty and the owner renders as a raw UUID until a full remount. FIX by mount-gating instead of closure-gating: render the editor half as a separate child component mounted ONLY when canEdit is true (e.g. `{canEdit ? <OwnerEditor .../> : <owner display>}`), with its own useAsyncData(listAdminUsers) that fetches on mount. That both fixes the race (component mounts when adminness becomes known) and keeps the non-admin-never-fetches guarantee structurally.

2. MEDIUM — AssetHealthLead calls listAssets() with no params; backend caps at 200 (default==max). With >200 assets the Monitored tile undercounts and a critical asset beyond page 1 is invisible, silently. Minimal honest fix: detect truncation (result length === 200) and render an explicit "showing the first 200 — open Assets for the full list" note on the widget; do NOT build multi-page aggregation.

3. LOW-MED — the "Needs attention" preview slices the backend's (namespace,name) alphabetical order; 5 warn-severity assets can hide a critical one behind "+N more". Sort the attention list by severity rank (critical > fail > warn, then has_failed_run) BEFORE the slice(0, 5).

4. LOW — OwnerBlock/OwnerEditor has no error state for a failed /admin/users fetch (degrades to an empty select, silently). Mirror the pattern you used in AssetHealthLead ("Asset health is unavailable right now.") — render a small inline error when state.status === 'error'.

5. LOW — reinstate the dropped `expect(listAdminUsers).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` assertion in the non-admin test: your harness supplies MeContext synchronously per-test, so canEdit is stable from first render and the assertion cannot plausibly flake there; if you saw bleed, fix the mock isolation (vi.clearAllMocks in afterEach is already present — ensure the mock module is per-test). With fix 1's mount-gating this invariant becomes structural, but keep the test pinning it. Also add a test for fix 1's recovery behavior: render with isAdmin=false-then-true if feasible with your harness (e.g. rerender with a new MeContext value) asserting the users fetch fires once adminness appears — if the harness genuinely can't express that, say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.

Gate: prettier, eslint, tsc, pnpm test:coverage (≥80 all-src). Push to your PR branch and reply with the fixes + gate results.
input.content:
The adversarial review of your PR #782 confirmed five findings. Fix all in-PR from your worktree (fixup commit + plain push — never amend/force-push), re-run the frontend gate, and report back with what you changed.

1. HIGH — OwnerBlock admin-users fetch race: useAsyncData's effect deps are [nonce] only, and your fetcher closes over `canEdit`. `useIsWorkspaceAdmin()` is false until /me resolves, and AuthGate renders children before /me resolves — so an admin deep-linking/refreshing into /assets/:id mounts OwnerBlock with canEdit=false, the fetch is skipped forever (no dep re-fires it, nothing calls its reload), the Select stays empty and the owner renders as a raw UUID until a full remount. FIX by mount-gating instead of closure-gating: render the editor half as a separate child component mounted ONLY when canEdit is true (e.g. `{canEdit ? <OwnerEditor .../> : <owner display>}`), with its own useAsyncData(listAdminUsers) that fetches on mount. That both fixes the race (component mounts when adminness becomes known) and keeps the non-admin-never-fetches guarantee structurally.

2. MEDIUM — AssetHealthLead calls listAssets() with no params; backend caps at 200 (default==max). With >200 assets the Monitored tile undercounts and a critical asset beyond page 1 is invisible, silently. Minimal honest fix: detect truncation (result length === 200) and render an explicit "showing the first 200 — open Assets for the full list" note on the widget; do NOT build multi-page aggregation.

3. LOW-MED — the "Needs attention" preview slices the backend's (namespace,name) alphabetical order; 5 warn-severity assets can hide a critical one behind "+N more". Sort the attention list by severity rank (critical > fail > warn, then has_failed_run) BEFORE the slice(0, 5).

4. LOW — OwnerBlock/OwnerEditor has no error state for a failed /admin/users fetch (degrades to an empty select, silently). Mirror the pattern you used in AssetHealthLead ("Asset health is unavailable right now.") — render a small inline error when state.status === 'error'.

5. LOW — reinstate the dropped `expect(listAdminUsers).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` assertion in the non-admin test: your harness supplies MeContext synchronously per-test, so canEdit is stable from first render and the assertion cannot plausibly flake there; if you saw bleed, fix the mock isolation (vi.clearAllMocks in afterEach is already present — ensure the mock module is per-test). With fix 1's mount-gating this invariant becomes structural, but keep the test pinning it. Also add a test for fix 1's recovery behavior: render with isAdmin=false-then-true if feasible with your harness (e.g. rerender with a new MeContext value) asserting the users fetch fires once adminness appears — if the harness genuinely can't express that, say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.

Gate: prettier, eslint, tsc, pnpm test:coverage (≥80 all-src). Push to your PR branch and reply with the fixes + gate results.

I'll land both once the fixups are pushed and green.

## What changed per finding

The non-admin-never-fetches guarantee is now structural (component never mounts).

2. **MEDIUM — truncation honesty in AssetHealthLead.** New `LIST_CAP = 200` constant; when `assets.length >= LIST_CAP` the widget renders "Showing the first 200 assets — open Assets for the full list." below the tiles (with a comment noting the tiles are a lower bound at the cap). No multi-page aggregation, per instruction.

3. **LOW-MED — attention preview severity ordering.** Added `SEVERITY_RANK` (critical 3 > fail 2 > warn 1 > operational-run-failure 0) and the attention list is `.filter(needsAttention).sort(worst-first)` before `slice(0, 5)` — stable sort preserves the backend's alphabetical order within a tier.

4. **LOW — OwnerBlock error state.** `state.status === 'error'` now renders "The user list is unavailable right now." inline (mirroring the AssetHealthLead pattern) and disables the Reassign button — no silently-empty picker.

5. **LOW — tests.** Reinstated `expect(listAdminUsers).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` in the non-admin test; mock isolation hardened by clearing mocks in `beforeEach` as well as `afterEach` (any late-recorded call between tests is wiped before counting). Added the **/me-race recovery test** — the harness *can* express it: extracted a `pageTree(isAdmin)` builder and used RTL `rerender(pageTree(true))` after asserting zero calls at `isAdmin=false`; the test asserts the fetch fires exactly once when adminness appears. Also added the user-list error-state test (message shown + button disabled), the severity-sort test (critical/fail lead the preview, trailing warns fold into "+2 more"), and truncation at-cap / below-cap tests.

## Gate results
- `prettier --check src tests` — clean
- `eslint src tests` — clean
- `tsc --noEmit` — clean

- Stability check: `AssetDetail.test.tsx` run 3× consecutively, 18/18 each time — the reinstated assertion did not flake (the earlier bleed is gone with mount-gating + beforeEach clearing)

Nothing deferred; all five findings resolved in-PR.</result>
